title: condition.v1 — unified condition node vocabulary
description: Single source of truth for the condition-node vocabulary shared by afb.deal.v2, afb.tradeplan.v2 and afb.alarm.v1 (alarms, trade plans and deals all evaluate the same operators identically). The root schema validates a bare conditionNode; deal.v2.json/tradeplan.v2.json/alarm.v1.json $ref into this file's $defs rather than duplicating them. Left/right pairing is enforced per source via oneOf: price node without `op` is a touch (level crossed between the previous and current sample); price node with a candle op (breakout/breakdown/crossing) requires `timeframe` and is evaluated only against the last CLOSED candle of that timeframe; price/quote node with a deprecated tick op (above/below/crosses_above/crosses_below/crossing, no timeframe) keeps legacy tick-based semantics. indicator and dataset nodes share one scalar vocabulary (above/below/crosses_above/crosses_below/crossing) comparing against a constant or another expression of the same kind.

scalarOp.description: above: cur > thr. below: cur < thr. crosses_above: prev <= prev_thr AND cur > thr. crosses_below: prev >= prev_thr AND cur < thr. crossing: crosses_above OR crosses_below. A touch without departure (cur == thr) is never a cross. For a constant right-hand side prev_thr == thr.

quoteExpr.description: deprecated (v1.4.0): quote (bid/ask) conditions are still accepted and evaluated, but are no longer offered by the AFB UI and will be removed in v2.0.0.

datasetExpr.description: position.* / orders / hhi / trades datasets share this shape; dataset_id=volume is declared here but temporarily unsupported by AFB/BF backends (see condition_semantics module docstring) and not offered by the UI.

oneOf.title: price touch (level)
oneOf.description: No `op`: the level is considered touched when it lies between the previous and current sample, i.e. min(prev, cur) <= level <= max(prev, cur). Equivalent to afb.deal.v1's only condition shape.

oneOf.title: price candle operator
oneOf.description: breakout: last CLOSED candle of `timeframe` has open < level AND close > level. breakdown: open > level AND close < level. crossing: breakout OR breakdown. Evaluated only on candle close, never intra-bar.

oneOf.title: price tick operator (deprecated)
oneOf.description: deprecated (v1.4.0): tick-by-tick price comparison without a timeframe, using the scalar vocabulary (see #/$defs/scalarOp). Still accepted and evaluated; prefer the touch shape (no op) or a candle operator with `timeframe`. Removal planned for v2.0.0.

oneOf.title: quote operator (deprecated)
oneOf.description: deprecated (v1.4.0): bid/ask conditions using the scalar vocabulary. Removal planned for v2.0.0.

oneOf.title: indicator operator
oneOf.description: Scalar comparison (see #/$defs/scalarOp) of an indicator against a constant or another indicator expression. `timeframe` is optional at the schema level (this same conditionNode is reused by afb.alarm.v1, where the timeframe lives on the alarm itself, not the node) but is REQUIRED by the BF executor for afb.deal.v2/afb.tradeplan.v2 indicator conditions — see condition_semantics module docstring. When present, it applies to BOTH sides of the comparison: left and right are evaluated on the same bar series. Indicator values are a (previous CLOSED bar, current FORMING bar) pair, matching evaluate_scalar_op's cur/prev semantics.

oneOf.title: dataset operator
oneOf.description: Scalar comparison (see #/$defs/scalarOp) of a dataset value (position.*, orders, hhi, trades) against a constant or another dataset expression of the same dataset_id.
